blocks.text: watched Marcus say it. He'd met a handful of people in his life who meant things the way Marcus meant them.
kept it. Marcus knew something was wrong with Cade, and he didn't know how wrong. Those were two different problems.
believed him. That was unusual — most people's reads of other people were optimistic self-deception. Marcus's felt like knowledge.
watched. He didn't put a name on what he was watching.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: How long have you known Nadia? Close friends?

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Wait — I asked last night, didn't I. You got quiet.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Nadia had a rough couple of years. You mentioned it.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Yeah. The quiet kind. Nothing that gets a name or anyone's sympathy — just the kind that piles up and you're not sure what you're looking at until it's all there. She's been carrying that for a while. I don't push. She talks when she's ready.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Memory like hers — holding onto things nobody else noticed — that's usually tied to caring about something very specifically.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: That tracks. Yeah. She holds on. She's always been like that — she'll remember something you said in passing six months later like she's been turning it over the whole time. Used to throw people who didn't know her.
He said it with complete warmth. He'd never considered it as anything other than a kind of loyalty.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: There you are. I knew you'd be out here.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: I know you weren't. I'm good. Drop it today.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: I know. I hear you. I'm saying leave it for today. One day. Can you do that?
He wasn't okay. But he knew Marcus would let him say he was. That was the ask — not for Marcus to believe it, just to let it stand.

blocks.prompt: Cade asked for one day. What is Julian reading in that?

options.label: Whatever it is, it's something Marcus already knows about. That's a specific kind of burden.
He's not just private. There's something he's actively trying not to think about.
He asked for space and Marcus gave it. That's real trust.
